Role

Platform Product Designer

Timeline

Aug 2024 – Present

Team

The work shown here is a representative sample — much of my work at Samsung is covered under NDA and cannot be publicly shared.

Samsung Research America

Complex Data Tools Platform UX Design Systems

Building the foundation for digital health at scale

Samsung's Digital Health Lab sits within Samsung Research America, focused on building the next generation of digital health tools that power Samsung's health ecosystem from wearables to platform-level health experiences.

I joined as the Platform Product Designer, working with a team of 2–3 designers — responsible for driving design direction, owning the design system, and leading multiple workstreams.

My Role

Leading design, building systems, growing a team

Design Leadership

Owning design direction

Setting the design vision and driving decisions across the health stack from early concepts to shipped features.

Design Systems

Building the foundation

Owning the design system end to end components, patterns, documentation ensuring consistency and scalability across the platform.

Team Management

Delegating & growing

Managing and mentoring a team of 2–3 designers, delegating workstreams, and running design reviews and critiques.

Highlights

Specific project details available on request happy to walk through the work in a conversation.

Project 01

Samsung Health Stack platform UX

Leading the end-to-end UX across Samsung's health platform defining information architecture, core interactions, and user flows that scale across the ecosystem.

Project 02

Design system ownership

Building and maintaining a scalable design system from component libraries to usage guidelines that empowers the team to ship consistently and quickly.

Reflections

What I'm learning

Samsung has pushed me to go deep across data visualization, health research tools, and enterprise solutions. Complex domains where design decisions have real downstream impact.

I've grown into fully owning the design space: setting direction, shaping the system, and being the connective tissue between product, eng, and research.

Owning the design system end to end has also sharpened how I think about systems thinking designing not just for one screen but for an entire ecosystem of experiences.
